questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
options: Both X and Y are in cis form
Both X and Y are in trans form
questions.solution: Co-ordination compound $(\mathrm{X})$ must be cis isomer having permanent dipole moment since it dissolves in polar solvents. Co-ordination compound $(\mathrm{Y})$ must be trans isomer which is non-polar since it dissolves in nonpolar solvents.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Total number of geometrical isomers of a $\mathrm{Ma}_3 \mathrm{~b}_3$ type of octahedral complex are
questions.solution: Only two geometrical isomers are possible for the complex $\mathrm{Ma}_3 \dot{\mathrm{~b}}_3$.
